requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall be applicable from immediate effect.
requirements.object: effect

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) These Directions shall be called the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India
(Credit Derivatives) Directions, 2026.
requirements.object: Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Credit Derivatives) Directions, 2026

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) These Directions shall apply to credit derivatives transactions undertaken in Over-
the-Counter (OTC) markets and on recognised stock exchanges in India.
requirements.object: transactions undertaken in Over- the-Counter (OTC) markets and on recognised stock exchanges in India

requirements.full_sentence: (iii) These Directions shall come into force on June 25, 2026.

(a) 'Auction settlement' of CDS means a settlement process in which the price of
the reference/deliverable obligation at which the settlement will happen is
determined through an auction mechanism.
requirements.object: which the price of the reference/deliverable obligation at which the settlement will happen is determined through an auction m

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) 'Cash settlement' of CDS means a settlement process in which the protection
seller pays to the protection buyer, the notional amount of the CDS contract less
the expected recovery value of the reference obligation.
requirements.object: which the protection seller pays to the protection buyer, the notional amount of the CDS contract less the expected recovery v

requirements.full_sentence:
(c) 'Central counterparty' means an entity that interposes itself between
counterparties to contracts traded in one or more financial markets, becoming
the buyer to every seller and the seller to every buyer and thereby ensuring the
performance of open contracts.
requirements.object: itself between counterparties to contracts traded in one or more financial markets, becoming the buyer to every seller and t

requirements.full_sentence:
(d) 'Company' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in Section 2(20) of the
Companies Act, 2013 (18 of 2013).
requirements.object: as assigned to it in Section 2(20) of the Companies Act, 2013 (18 of 2013)

requirements.full_sentence:
(e) 'Corporate bonds and debentures' mean non-convertible debt securities which
create or acknowledge indebtedness and include debentures, bonds and such
other securities issued by a body corporate or a trust or any statutory body
constituted by virtue of a legislation, whether constituting a charge on the assets
of the issuer or not, but excludes money market debt instruments, security
receipts, securitised debt instruments and bonds issued by the Central
Government or a State Government, or such other persons as may be specified
by the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
(f) 'Credit Default Swap (CDS)' means a credit derivative contract in which one
counterparty (protection seller) commits to pay to the other counterparty
(protection buyer) in the case of a credit event with respect to a reference entity
and in return, the protection buyer makes periodic payments (premium) to the
protection seller until the maturity of the contract or the credit event, whichever
is earlier.
requirements.object: in which one counterparty (protection seller) commits to pay to the other counterparty (protection buyer) in the case of

requirements.full_sentence:
(g) 'Credit derivative' means a derivative contract whose value is derived from the
credit risk of an underlying debt instrument or from an index of underlying debt
instruments.
requirements.object: value is derived from the credit risk of an underlying debt instrument or from an index of underlying debt instruments

requirements.full_sentence:
(h) 'Credit event' means a pre-defined event in a credit derivative contract, which
triggers a settlement under the contract.
requirements.object: a credit derivative contract, which triggers a settlement under the contract

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) 'Deliverable obligation' means a debt instrument issued by the reference entity
that the protection buyer can deliver to the protection seller in a physically settled
CDS contract, in case of occurrence of a credit event.
requirements.object: by the reference entity that the protection buyer can deliver to the protection seller in a physically settled CDS contract,

requirements.full_sentence:
(j) 'Electronic Trading Platform (ETP)' shall have the same meaning as assigned to
it in Paragraph 2 (iii) of Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Electronic
Trading Platforms) Directions, 2025 dated January 16, 2025, as amended from
time to time.
requirements.object: as assigned to it in Paragraph 2 (iii) of Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Electronic Trading Platforms) Directions, 20

requirements.full_sentence:
(k) 'Exchange' shall mean 'recognised stock exchange' and shall have the same
meaning as assigned to it in Section 2(f) of the Securities Contract Regulation
Act, 1956 (42 of 1956).
requirements.object: and shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in Section 2(f) of the Securities Contract Regulation Act, 1956 (42

requirements.full_sentence:
(l) 'Future on credit indices' means a standardised derivative contract, traded on a
recognised stock exchange, to buy or sell an index of underlying debt
instruments at a specified future date, at a price determined at the time of the
contract.
requirements.object: traded on a recognised stock exchange, to buy or sell an index of underlying debt instruments at a specified futur

requirements.full_sentence:
(m) 'Government-related entity' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in
Paragraph 9 of Indian Accounting Standard (Ind AS) 24: Related Party
Disclosures.
requirements.object: as assigned to it in Paragraph 9 of Indian Accounting Standard (Ind AS) 24: Related Party Disclosures

requirements.full_sentence:
(n) 'Hedging' means the activity of undertaking a credit derivative transaction to
reduce credit risk of a particular debt instrument or a portfolio of debt
instruments.
requirements.object: a credit derivative transaction to reduce credit risk of a particular debt instrument or a portfolio of debt instruments

requirements.full_sentence:
(o) 'Infrastructure company' means a company engaged primarily in activities
related to specific infrastructure sub-sectors listed under the Harmonized Master
List of Infrastructure Sub-sectors notified by Department of Economic Affairs,
Ministry of Finance, Government of India, via gazette notification no.
requirements.object: in activities related to specific infrastructure sub-sectors listed under the Harmonized Master List of Infrastructure Sub

requirements.full_sentence:
(p) 'Market-maker' means an entity which provides prices to users and other market-
makers.
requirements.object: prices to users and other market- makers

requirements.full_sentence:
(r) 'Net worth' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in Section 2(57) of the
Companies Act, 2013 (18 of 2013).
requirements.object: as assigned to it in Section 2(57) of the Companies Act, 2013 (18 of 2013)

requirements.full_sentence:
(s) 'Over-the-Counter (OTC) markets' mean the markets where transactions are
undertaken in any manner other than on exchanges and shall include electronic
trading platforms (ETPs).
requirements.object: (ETPs)

requirements.full_sentence:
(t) 'Person resident outside India' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in
Section 2(w) of Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (42 of 1999).
requirements.object: as assigned to it in Section 2(w) of Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (42 of 1999)

requirements.full_sentence:
(u) 'Physical settlement' of CDS means a settlement process in which the protection
buyer delivers any of the eligible deliverable obligations to the protection seller
against the receipt of notional amount of the CDS contract.
requirements.object: which the protection buyer delivers any of the eligible deliverable obligations to the protection seller against the receipt o

requirements.full_sentence:
(v) 'Reference asset' means a debt instrument issued by a reference entity or an
index of underlying debt instruments and specified in a total return swap contract.
requirements.object: by a reference entity or an index of underlying debt instruments and specified in a total return swap contract

requirements.full_sentence:
(w) 'Reference entity' means an entity, against whose credit risk, a credit derivative
contract is entered into.
requirements.object: credit risk, a credit derivative contract is entered into

requirements.full_sentence:
(x) 'Reference obligation' means a debt instrument issued by the reference entity
and specified in a CDS contract for the purpose of valuation of the contract and
for determining the cash settlement value or the deliverable obligation in case of
occurrence of a credit event.
requirements.object: by the reference entity and specified in a CDS contract for the purpose of valuation of the contract and for determining the

requirements.full_sentence:
(y) 'Related party' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under Paragraph
9 of Indian Accounting Standard (Ind AS) 24 - Related Party Disclosures.
requirements.object: as assigned to it under Paragraph 9 of Indian Accounting Standard (Ind AS) 24 - Related Party Disclosures

requirements.full_sentence:
(z) 'Resident' shall mean a 'person resident in India' and shall have the same
meaning as assigned to it in section 2(v) of the Foreign Exchange Management
Act, 1999 (42 of 1999).
requirements.object: in India' and shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in section 2(v) of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (42 o

requirements.full_sentence:
(aa) 'Single-name CDS' means a CDS contract in which the underlying is a single
reference entity.
requirements.object: which the underlying is a single reference entity

requirements.full_sentence:
(bb) 'Substitution event' means any event that results in the reference obligation
being replaced by another obligation issued by the reference entity.
requirements.object: in the reference obligation being replaced by another obligation issued by the reference entity

requirements.full_sentence:
(cc) 'Succession event' means any event that results in the reference entity no longer
being the primary obligor for the reference obligation.
requirements.object: in the reference entity no longer being the primary obligor for the reference obligation

requirements.full_sentence:
(dd) 'Total Return Swap (TRS)' means a credit derivative contract under which one
counterparty (total return payer) commits to transfer the entire economic
performance of a reference asset to the other counterparty (total return receiver),
and, in return, receives a pre-determined fixed or floating rate linked to a
benchmark.
requirements.object: under which one counterparty (total return payer) commits to transfer the entire economic performance of a reference asse

requirements.full_sentence:
(ee) 'User' means a person that undertakes derivative transactions other than as a
market-maker.
requirements.object: derivative transactions other than as a market-maker

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Words and expressions, used but not defined in these Directions, shall have the
same meaning as assigned to them in the Act or in FEMA, 1999.
requirements.object: as assigned to them in the Act or in FEMA, 1999

requirements.full_sentence: (i) The following persons shall be eligible to participate in credit derivatives market:
requirements.object: in credit derivatives market:

requirements.full_sentence:
Directions on credit derivatives in the OTC market
Note: An index which is based wholly or partially on money market debt instruments
shall be published by a financial benchmark administrator which is duly authorised
by the Reserve Bank under the Reserve Bank of India (Financial Benchmark
Administrators) Directions, 2023 dated December 28, 2023, as amended from time
to time.
requirements.object: financial benchmark administrator which is duly authorised by the Reserve Bank under the Reserve Bank of India (Financial Benchmark

requirements.full_sentence:
4.1 Permitted products
Market-makers and users may undertake transactions in CDS and TRS contracts, subject
to the directions specified hereinafter.
requirements.object: and TRS contracts

requirements.full_sentence: (i) The following entities shall be eligible to act as market-makers in credit derivatives:
requirements.object: as market-makers in credit derivatives:

conditions: In case an NBFC fails to meet the eligibility criteria for acting as a market-maker
requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) In case an NBFC fails to meet the eligibility criteria for acting as a market-maker, it
shall cease to act as a market-maker.
requirements.object: a market-maker

requirements.full_sentence:
The NBFC shall continue to meet all its
obligations under the existing contracts till the maturity/termination of such contracts.
requirements.object: its obligations under the existing contracts till the maturity/termination of such contracts

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) At least one of the parties to a credit derivative transaction shall be a market-maker
or a central counterparty authorised by the Reserve Bank for the purpose.
requirements.object: a central counterparty authorised by the Reserve Bank for the purpose

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Users shall be classified by market-makers either as retail or non-retail for the purpose
of offering credit derivative contracts.
requirements.object: either as retail or non-retail for the purpose of offering credit derivative contracts

requirements.full_sentence: (ii) The following users shall be eligible to be classified as non-retail users:
requirements.object: classified as non-retail users:

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Any user who is not eligible to be classified as a non-retail user shall be classified as
a retail user.
requirements.object: retail user

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Any user who is otherwise eligible to be classified as a non-retail user shall have the
option to get classified as a retail user.
requirements.object: get classified as a retail user

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) A resident retail user, other than an individual, shall be allowed to buy protection only
for the purpose of hedging.
requirements.object: protection only for the purpose of hedging

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) A non-retail user shall be allowed to buy protection without any restriction in terms of
purpose.
requirements.object: protection without any restriction in terms of purpose

requirements.full_sentence: (iii) The following non-retail users shall be eligible to act as protection sellers:
requirements.object: as protection sellers:

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Insurance Companies, Pension Funds, Mutual Funds and Alternative Investment
Funds mentioned under Paragraph 4.3.1(iii) shall be permitted to act as protection
sellers subject to approval of their respective regulator.
requirements.object: as protection sellers subject to approval of their respective regulator

requirements.full_sentence: (v) Participation by FPIs in CDS contracts shall be subject to the following,
requirements.object: following,

requirements.full_sentence:
(a) The notional amount of CDS protection sold by all FPIs shall be subject to a limit
of 5 per cent of the outstanding stock of corporate bonds.
requirements.object: limit of 5 per cent of the outstanding stock of corporate bonds

requirements.full_sentence:
(CCIL) shall disseminate the utilisation of the limit based on the
reporting by the market makers in terms of Paragraph 4.5.5 of these directions
and reporting by stock exchanges in terms of Paragraph 6.1 (vii) of these
directions.
requirements.object: the limit based on the reporting by the market makers in terms of Paragraph 4

requirements.full_sentence: FPIs shall not sell any further CDS protection once the limit is utilised.
requirements.object: protection once the limit is utilised

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) Debt instruments received by FPIs as deliverable obligation and debt instruments
purchased by FPIs for meeting deliverable obligation in physical settlement of
CDS contracts shall be reckoned under the investment limits for corporate bonds
as specified in in A.P.
requirements.object: investment limits for corporate bonds as specified in in A

conditions:
In case of non-availability of investment limit at the
time of physical settlement
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of non-availability of investment limit at the
time of physical settlement, such debt instruments shall be adjusted against the
revised limit in the subsequent review of investment limit.
requirements.object: revised limit in the subsequent review of investment limit

requirements.full_sentence:
(c) The notional amount of protection sold by FPIs, and the debt instruments
received as deliverable obligation as well as debt instruments purchased for
meeting deliverable obligation by FPIs in physical settlement of CDS contracts
shall not be subject to the minimum residual maturity requirement or issue-wise
limit applicable to FPI investment in corporate bonds as specified in Master
Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Non-resident Investment in Debt Instruments)
Directions, 2025, dated January 7, 2025, as amended from time to time.
requirements.object: minimum residual maturity requirement or issue-wise limit applicable to FPI investment in corporate bonds as specified in Master Dir

requirements.full_sentence:
(d) A market maker shall not offer a CDS contract to an FPI where the reference
obligation or the index includes a money market debt instrument or a debt
instrument with residual maturity less than one year or a bond with call / put
options exercisable within one year from the date of the undertaking the CDS
contract.
requirements.object: to an FPI where the reference obligation or the index includes a money market debt instrument or a debt instrument with residual

requirements.full_sentence: (vi) A market-maker shall not offer CDS contracts to an individual.
requirements.object: an individual

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) A market-maker may offer a TRS contract to a resident retail user, other than an
individual, only for the purpose of hedging.
requirements.object: to a resident retail user, other than an individual, only for the purpose of hedging

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) A market-maker may offer a TRS contract to a resident non-retail user without any
restriction in terms of purpose.
requirements.object: to a resident non-retail user without any restriction in terms of purpose

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) A market-maker may offer a TRS contract to a person outside India subject to the
following:
requirements.object: to a person outside India subject to the following:

requirements.full_sentence:
(a) A market-maker may offer a TRS contract to a person resident outside India,
including an FPI, for the purpose of hedging;
requirements.object: to a person resident outside India, including an FPI, for the purpose of hedging

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) A market maker may also offer a TRS contract to a person resident outside
India, including an FPI, wherein the person resident outside India is the total
return receiver provided that the TRS is offered as a fully funded structure in
which the person resident outside India provides to the market-maker in India
the full notional amount of the reference asset.
requirements.object: as a fully funded structure in which the person resident outside India provides to the market-maker in India the full notional amou

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that no offshore derivative instrument with the TRS contract as
underlying shall be written by the person resident outside India.
requirements.object: person resident outside India

requirements.full_sentence:
(c) A market maker may offer a TRS contract to a person resident outside India,
including an FPI, directly or on a back-to-back basis through overseas entities
(including overseas branches, IFSC Banking Units, wholly owned subsidiaries
and joint ventures of market-makers) subject to the following conditions:
i.
requirements.object: to a person resident outside India, including an FPI, directly or on a back-to-back basis through overseas entities (including overseas branches

requirements.full_sentence:
The market-maker shall provide information, data or any other particulars
required by the Reserve Bank of India in respect of the aforesaid
transactions in the manner and time prescribed.
requirements.object: any other particulars required by the Reserve Bank of India in respect of the aforesaid transactions in the manner and ti

requirements.full_sentence:
(d) The notional amount of the TRS contract offered to a person resident outside
India through a fully funded structure shall be reckoned under the investment
limits for corporate bonds as specified in Master Direction - Reserve Bank of
India (Non-resident Investment in Debt Instruments) Directions, 2025, dated
January 7, 2025, as amended from time to time;
requirements.object: investment limits for corporate bonds as specified in Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Non-resident Investment in Debt

conditions:
Where the reference asset specified in a TRS contract includes an unlisted
corporate bond or debenture
requirements.full_sentence:
(e) Where the reference asset specified in a TRS contract includes an unlisted
corporate bond or debenture, the same shall comply with the end use
restrictions set out in Paragraph 4.4 (vi) of the Master Direction - Reserve Bank
of India (Non-resident Investment in Debt Instruments) Directions, 2025, dated
January 7, 2025;
requirements.object: use restrictions set out in Paragraph 4

requirements.full_sentence:
(f) A market maker shall not offer a TRS contract to a person resident outside India
where the reference asset or the index includes a money market debt
instrument or a debt instrument with residual maturity less than one year or a
bond with call / put options exercisable within one year from the date of the
contract;
requirements.object: to a person resident outside India where the reference asset or the index includes a money market debt instrument or a debt instr

requirements.full_sentence: (iv) A market-maker shall not offer a TRS contract to an individual.
requirements.object: to an individual

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) The reference entity in a credit derivative contract shall be a resident entity who is
eligible to issue any of the debt instruments mentioned under Paragraph 4.4 (ii).
requirements.object: who is eligible to issue any of the debt instruments mentioned under Paragraph 4

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The following debt instruments issued in India shall be eligible to be a reference
obligation in a CDS contract or a reference asset in a TRS contract:
requirements.object: a reference obligation in a CDS contract or a reference asset in a TRS contract:

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Bonds with call/put options shall be eligible to be reference obligations/reference
assets.
requirements.object: reference obligations/reference assets

requirements.full_sentence:
shall not be permitted
as reference obligations/reference assets.
requirements.object: obligations/reference assets

requirements.full_sentence:
(v) The reference obligation /deliverable obligation /reference asset shall be in
dematerialised form.

(vi) The underlying reference obligation for a CDS or a reference asset for a TRS may
be an index comprising solely of eligible debt instruments as set out in Paragraph 4.4
requirements.object: solely of eligible debt instruments as set out in Paragraph 4

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Market participants shall not enter into CDS or TRS transactions if the reference entity
is a related party to either counterparty.
requirements.object: TRS transactions if the reference entity is a related party to either counterparty

requirements.full_sentence:
However, two (or more) government-related
entities shall not be deemed as related parties for the purpose of these Directions.
requirements.object: parties for the purpose of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Unless specifically permitted in these directions, market participants shall not
undertake credit derivative transactions involving reference entities/reference
obligations//reference assets, if there are regulatory restrictions on such participants
assuming similar exposures in the cash market or in violation of any other regulatory
restriction, as may be applicable.
requirements.object: involving reference entities/reference obligations//reference assets

requirements.full_sentence:
However, provisions under Paragraph 2,
Paragraph 5.1 and Paragraph 5.2 of the above circular shall not apply to credit
derivative transactions undertaken in terms of these Directions.
requirements.object: transactions undertaken in terms of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Market participants shall settle their credit derivative contracts bilaterally or through
any clearing and settlement arrangement approved by the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: contracts bilaterally or through any clearing and settlement arrangement approved by the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The procedure for cash settlement and auction
settlement of CDS contracts shall be determined by the Credit Derivatives
Determinations Committee, as specified under Paragraph 5 of these Directions.
requirements.object: Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee, as specified under Paragraph 5 of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
TRS
contracts undertaken by a person resident outside India, who is not an FPI, shall be
cash settled.

(vi) Any floating interest rate used in a TRS shall be a benchmark published by a financial
benchmark administrator which is duly authorised by the Reserve Bank under the
Reserve Bank of India (Financial Benchmark Administrators) Directions, 2023 dated
December 28, 2023, as amended from time to time.
requirements.object: by a financial benchmark administrator which is duly authorised by the Reserve Bank under the Reserve Bank of India (Financia

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) While offering a credit derivative contract for the purpose of hedging, the market
maker shall ensure that the user:

(a) shall have exposure to the reference asset / any of the eligible reference
obligations/ any one or more of the debt instruments comprising the index in the
case of a credit derivative contract on an index
requirements.object: reference asset / any of the eligible reference obligations/ any one or more of the debt instruments comprising the index in the

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) shall not buy a credit derivative contract(s) with notional amount higher than the
face value of the reference obligation / reference asset held by them;
requirements.object: contract(s) with notional amount higher than the face value of the reference obligation / reference asset held by them

requirements.full_sentence:
(c) shall not buy a credit derivative contract with a tenor later than the maturity of
the reference obligation / reference asset held by them or the standard CDS /
TRS maturity date immediately after the maturity of the reference obligation or
reference asset.
requirements.object: contract with a tenor later than the maturity of the reference obligation / reference asset held by them or the standard CDS /

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) To ensure compliance with the above, market-makers may call for any relevant
information/documents from the user, who, in turn, shall be obliged to provide such
information.
requirements.object: such information

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Users undertaking credit derivative transactions for the purpose of hedging shall exit
their credit derivative position within one month from the date they cease to have the
underlying exposure.
requirements.object: position within one month from the date they cease to have the underlying exposure

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) The settlement basis and market conventions for credit derivative contracts shall be
specified by the Fixed Income Money Market and Derivatives Association of India
(FIMMDA), in consultation with market participants and based on international best
practices.
requirements.object: Fixed Income Money Market and Derivatives Association of India (FIMMDA), in consultation with market participants and based on int

requirements.full_sentence:
FIMMDA may also prescribe standard documentation procedures for credit
derivative transactions.
requirements.object: procedures for credit derivative transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
Market participants may, alternatively, use a standard master
agreement for credit derivative contracts.
requirements.object: standard master agreement for credit derivative contracts

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Market participants shall ensure that the CDS contract represents a direct claim on
the protection seller.
requirements.object: contract represents a direct claim on the protection seller

requirements.full_sentence: The contract shall not have any clause that may:
requirements.object: may:

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Market-makers in OTC markets shall comply with the Master Direction - Reserve
Bank of India (Market-makers in OTC Derivatives) Directions, 2021 issued vide RBI
Circular No.
requirements.object: Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Market-makers in OTC Derivatives) Directions, 2021 issued vide RBI Circular No

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Market-makers shall report all OTC credit derivative transactions within 30 minutes
of the transaction, to the trade repository of Clearing Corporation of India Ltd.
requirements.object: derivative transactions within 30 minutes of the transaction, to the trade repository of Clearing Corporation of India Ltd

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Market-makers shall report all amendments, unwinding, novation, settlement
transactions, and any credit, substitution or succession event to the trade repository
of CCIL.
requirements.object: novation, settlement transactions, and any credit, substitution or succession event to the trade repository of CCIL

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) The reporting formats shall be as indicated by CCIL with the prior approval of the
Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: CCIL with the prior approval of the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) FIMMDA shall set up a Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee, consisting of
market-makers and users in credit derivatives as voting members.
requirements.object: Derivatives Determinations Committee, consisting of market-makers and users in credit derivatives as voting members

requirements.full_sentence:
FIMMDA shall
ensure that users are adequately represented in the Committee.
requirements.object: adequately represented in the Committee

requirements.full_sentence:
The Committee may
also include central counterparties as observer members and legal/audit/consultancy
firms as consultative members.
requirements.object: as observer members and legal/audit/consultancy firms as consultative members

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) FIMMDA shall establish rules for governing the activities of the Credit Derivatives
Determinations Committee in line with international best practices.
requirements.object: the activities of the Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee in line with international best practices

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) The Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee, when approached by market
participants, shall make factual determinations regarding key provisions of credit
derivative contracts including, but not limited to, the occurrence of a credit event,
substitution event, succession event, determining the identity of successor reference
entity, etc.
requirements.object: key provisions of credit derivative contracts including, but not limited to, the occurrence of a credit event, s

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) The Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee shall, in consultation with market
participants, develop a standard procedure for cash and auction settlement of CDS
contracts.
requirements.object: market participants, develop a standard procedure for cash and auction settlement of CDS contracts

requirements.full_sentence:
(v) The Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee, when approached by market
participants, may conduct an auction to determine the reference price for settlement
of CDS contracts.
requirements.object: determine the reference price for settlement of CDS contracts

requirements.full_sentence:
The Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee shall put in
place procedures/safeguards to ensure that the reference price is determined in a
fair and transparent manner.
requirements.object: to ensure that the reference price is determined in a fair and transparent manner

requirements.full_sentence:
(vi) The decisions of the Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee shall be binding
on the market participants.
requirements.object: market participants

requirements.full_sentence:
Directions for exchange traded credit derivatives
Note: An index which is based wholly or partially on money market debt instruments
shall be published by a financial benchmark administrator which is duly authorised
by the Reserve Bank under the Reserve Bank of India (Financial Benchmark
Administrators) Directions, 2023 dated December 28, 2023, as amended from time
to time.
requirements.object: financial benchmark administrator which is duly authorised by the Reserve Bank under the Reserve Bank of India (Financial Benchmark

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: A credit index which is based wholly or partially on money market debt
instruments shall be published by a financial benchmark administrator which is duly
authorised by the Reserve Bank under the Reserve Bank of India (Financial
Benchmark Administrators) Directions, 2023 dated December 28, 2023, as amended
from time to time.
requirements.object: financial benchmark administrator which is duly authorised by the Reserve Bank under the Reserve Bank of India (Financial Benchmark

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Exchanges may offer standardised single-name CDS contracts and CDS contracts
on credit indices with guaranteed settlement.
requirements.object: contracts and CDS contracts on credit indices with guaranteed settlement

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Exchanges shall obtain prior approval of the Reserve Bank for product design,
changes in product design, eligible participants and other details of CDS contracts.
requirements.object: the Reserve Bank for product design, changes in product design, eligible participants and other details of CDS contracts

requirements.full_sentence:
The operational guidelines on the procedure for execution and settlement of trades
on stock exchanges shall be prescribed by SEBI.

(iii) The reference entities and reference obligations for exchange-traded CDS shall be
as specified under Paragraph 4.4 of these Directions.
requirements.object: Paragraph 4

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) The credit index for an exchange-traded CDS contract shall be an index comprising
solely of eligible debt instruments as set out in para 4.4 (ii) of these Directions,
subject to the condition that the index is published by a financial benchmark
administrator which is duly authorised by the Reserve Bank under the Reserve Bank
of India (Financial Benchmark Administrators) Directions, 2023 dated December 28,
2023, as amended from time to time, or that the index is composed / administered
as per the directions issued by SEBI from time to time.
requirements.object: solely of eligible debt instruments as set out in para 4.4 (ii) of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Exchanges may offer futures contracts on credit indices with guaranteed settlement,
provided that:

(a) The credit index used as underlying for futures contracts shall be composed
solely of eligible debt instruments as set out in para 4.4 (ii) of these directions;
requirements.object: eligible debt instruments as set out in para 4

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) The composition / administration of the index shall be as per the directions
issued by SEBI.
requirements.object: directions issued by SEBI

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Exchanges shall ensure that the participants on exchanges are made adequately
aware of the risks associated with credit derivative contracts.
requirements.object: on exchanges are made adequately aware of the risks associated with credit derivative contracts

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Participants shall not undertake credit derivative transactions involving reference
entities if there are regulatory restrictions on assuming similar exposures in the cash
market or in violation of any other regulatory restriction, as may be applicable.
requirements.object: involving reference entities if there are regulatory restrictions on assuming similar exposures in the cash m

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Exchanges shall furnish any information relating to credit derivative transactions to
the Reserve Bank or any other agency as may be specified by the Reserve Bank in
the manner and format and within the time frame as may be specified by the Reserve
Bank.
requirements.object: to credit derivative transactions to the Reserve Bank or any other agency as may be specified by the Reserve Bank in

requirements.full_sentence:
Valuation methodology
Market-makers shall put in place robust methodologies for marking to market credit
derivative contracts.
requirements.object: methodologies for marking to market credit derivative contracts

requirements.full_sentence:
The valuation methodology adopted shall be applied consistently
and shall be appropriately documented and disclosed.
requirements.object: shall be appropriately documented and disclosed

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Market participants shall follow the applicable prudential norms and capital adequacy
requirements for credit derivatives issued by their respective regulators.
requirements.object: norms and capital adequacy requirements for credit derivatives issued by their respective regulators

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The accounting of credit derivative contracts by market participants shall be as per
notified and applicable accounting standards read with regulatory
guidelines/instructions issued by the respective regulators.
requirements.object: and applicable accounting standards read with regulatory guidelines/instructions issued by the respective regulators

requirements.full_sentence:
In case the notified
applicable accounting standards or the respective regulator have not prescribed the
accounting treatment for credit derivative contracts, guidance, if any, issued by the
Institute of Chartered Accountants of India shall be followed in this regard.
requirements.object: regard

requirements.full_sentence:
Obligation to provide information sought by the Reserve Bank
The Reserve Bank may call for any information or statement or seek any clarification,
which in the opinion of the Reserve Bank is relevant, from persons or agencies dealing
in credit derivatives contracts, including eligible participants, and such persons, agencies
and participants shall furnish such information, statement or clarification in the manner
and format and within the time frame as may be specified by the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: or clarification in the manner and format and within the time frame as may be specified by the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) The Reserve Bank, or any other person or agency authorised by the Reserve Bank,
may in public interest, publish any anonymised data related to transactions in credit
derivatives market.
requirements.object: any anonymised data related to transactions in credit derivatives market

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) CCIL shall publish the notional value of credit derivative contracts outstanding for
each debt instrument on a daily basis.
requirements.object: of credit derivative contracts outstanding for each debt instrument on a daily basis

requirements.full_sentence:
Violation of Directions
In the event of any person or agency violating any provision of these Directions or the
provisions of any other applicable law, the Reserve Bank may, in addition to taking any
penal or regulatory action in accordance with law, disallow that person or agency from
dealing in the credit derivatives market for a period not exceeding one month at a time,
after providing reasonable opportunity to the person or agency to defend its actions, and
such action will be made public by the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: taking any penal or regulatory action in accordance with law, disallow that person or agency from dealing in the credit derivatives m

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall apply to all credit derivative transactions entered into from the
date the Directions come into effect.
requirements.object: derivative transactions entered into from the date the Directions come into effect
